The Gray Lady's agents watch.","tags":["region","mistlands","fog","bells"]},{"id":145,"title":"Silkstrand","category":"regions","body":"Silkstrand is a city of bridges and canals. Power flows through silk, silver, and shadow. The Matron rules, but the Ragman, Tide Father, and Silk Mother hold the underworld. Favours are measured in threads, and the ninth bridge leads to the Sunken Quarter.","tags":["region","silkstrand","city","trade"]},{"id":146,"title":"Thepyrgos","category":"regions","body":"Thepyrgos is the City of a Thousand Stairs, a coastal academic city. The Synod licenses magic; the Chain-Lanterns hunt untutored talent. The Unfinished Stair appears to those who ask forbidden questions. The Witness and the Ninth war in forgotten archives.","tags":["region","thepyrgos","stairs","synod"]},{"id":147,"title":"Valewood","category":"regions","body":"The Valewood is an ancient forest of fading empires, fae courts, and beast-kin. Lethai-al and Lethai-thora guard star-roads and sealed vats. The Hazel Queen and Alder King rule the courts. The Ninth Leaf falls to hidden thresholds.","tags":["region","valewood","forest","fae"]},{"id":148,"title":"Ykrul (Orcs)","category":"regions","body":"Ykrul are steppe nomads of the Violet Steppe. Honour measured in oaths and remounts. They ride between wolf standards. Kon'reh is their game of encirclement. They count in eights, fearing the ninth. The Khagan's Court rules from the saddle.","tags":["region","orcs","ykrul","steppe"]},{"id":149,"title":"Armor Conversion","category":"rules","body":"Armor converts Harm to Fatigue. Light: Harm 1 → Fatigue 1, Harm 2 → Harm 1 + Fatigue 1, Harm 3 → Harm 2 + Fatigue 1. Medium: Harm 1 → Fatigue 1, Harm 2 → Fatigue 1, Harm 3 → Harm 2 + Fatigue 1. Heavy: Harm 1 → Fatigue 1, Harm 2 → Fatigue 1, Harm 3 → Fatigue 2. Minimum 1 Fatigue per hit.","tags":["armor","conversion","srd"]},{"id":150,"title":"Boons","category":"rules","body":"Boons are narrative tokens. Earn 1 on Partial, 2 on Miss. Max 5, trim to 2 at scene end. Spend 1 to: re-roll a die, improve Position, activate an Asset, or convert 2 Boons to 1 XP (once per session, max 2 XP).","tags":["boons","resource","srd"]},{"id":151,"title":"Core Resolution","category":"rules","body":"Every important action follows the loop: declare action and approach, GM sets Position and DV, roll dice pool (Attribute + Skill d10s), count successes (6+ = 1 success, 10 = 2 successes), count Story Beats (1s = SB for GM), compare to DV, apply outcome from the Outcome Matrix.","tags":["resolution","core","srd"]},{"id":152,"title":"Difficulty Value (DV)","category":"rules","body":"DV determines target successes needed. 2 = routine, 3 = default, 4 = hard, 5+ = extreme. Default DV is 3.","tags":["dv","difficulty","srd"]},{"id":153,"title":"Fatigue and Harm","category":"rules","body":"Fatigue track size = Body. Each point worsens Position; if already Desperate, -1 die per Fatigue. Overflow fills track → increase Harm by 1, clear Fatigue. Harm levels: 1 (–1 die), 2 (–1 die most actions), 3 (incapacitated). Taking Harm clears existing Fatigue first.","tags":["fatigue","harm","health","srd"]},{"id":154,"title":"Outcome Matrix","category":"rules","body":"Successes ≥ DV, SB = 0 → Clean Success. Successes ≥ DV, SB > 0 → Success with SB (GM spends SB for complication). 0 < Successes < DV → Partial (gain 1 Boon). Successes = 0 → Miss (gain 2 Boons, GM escalates).","tags":["outcome","matrix","srd"]},{"id":155,"title":"Position","category":"rules","body":"Position reflects risk: Dominant (re-roll one failure), Controlled (no re-rolls), Desperate (re-roll one success). 10s are never re-rolled. Spending 1 Boon improves Position by one step.","tags":["position","risk","srd"]},{"id":156,"title":"Story Beats (SB)","category":"rules","body":"Each die showing a 1 gives the GM 1 Story Beat. Spend SB to introduce complications: 1 SB = minor (noise, tick timer), 2 SB = moderate (alarm, lesser foe), 3 SB = serious (reinforcements, gear breaks), 4+ SB = major (trap springs, scene shift).","tags":["story beats","sb","gm","srd"]},{"id":157,"title":"Always Another Exit","category":"talents","body":"Once per session, declare a prepared escape route during a heist or when cornered. The GM may impose a cost, but you get out.","tags":["talent","rogue","stealth"],"cost":4},{"id":158,"title":"Arcane Dominance","category":"talents","body":"When contesting another magical effect, automatically overpower weaker effects (Tier II or lower). At 4 segments: Navigation rolls in Acasia gain +1 DV (roads shift). At 8 segments: the Curse Stirrs complication triggers once per session (roll on the Curse Stirrs table). On failure, you gain the Condition Debtor to the Dead (any spirit can demand one service from you). On a critical failure, the Curse Awakening timer ticks +2 immediately. It is a threshold of death and memory, where the Empire's last toll is still collected.","tags":["acasia","location","road","curse"]},{"id":236,"title":"Free Companies (Acasia)","category":"factions","body":"Mercenary bands that are the only law between the Margravine and the Lame King. They trade loyalty like grain, swapping colors mid-march. Their goal: find a permanent contract before winter. Their method: selling loyalty, swapping sides for better pay. Why players care: They are the only armed force that can be hired, but they are unreliable – they may flip mid-battle, and breaking a contract with them ticks the Curse.","tags":["acasia","faction","mercenary"]},{"id":237,"title":"Hedge-witches (Acasia)","category":"lore","body":"Practitioners of threshold magic who tie red thread around bridges and leave offerings of salt at crossroads to keep the Curse from spreading. The Margravine tolerates them; the Lame King ignores them; the Curse listens when they speak.","tags":["acasia","witchcraft","hedge","curse"]},{"id":238,"title":"The Ninth (Acasia)","category":"lore","body":"In Acasia, the ninth toll paid at a bridge is never the last. The wise pay eight and barter for the ninth. The ninth toll is collected by a child with no name – the Curse's enforcer. To count to nine, speak the ninth name, or pour the ninth cup is to invite the Hollow's attention. The Ninth Taboo is a universal warning across cultures, but in Acasia it is a living law enforced by the land itself.","tags":["acasia","ninth","taboo","curse"]}]

summary:
**Campaign Summary:**  
You are in the **Rusty Tankard**, a tense tavern in an unnamed city. Key characters include **Sera Vex**, a scarred woman offering silver for a cask from the old aqueducts; **Rik**, a wiry knife-wielding man spooked by Sera; the **barkeep**, a weathered informant; and **Tomas**, a drain worker who knows the tunnels. You paid Tomas **three silver** to guide you personally.  

**Current situation:** A mysterious note, mimicking your handwriting, claims the cask is a decoy and the real prize lies in an **old chapel beneath the North Gate**. The note warns Sera is a liar and includes a sketch of the **three-ring crest** Sera described. The barkeep confirms Sera "doesn't leave loose ends."  

**Unresolved threads:**  
- Who sent the note, and why?  
- Is Sera betraying you, or is the note a trap?  
- The aqueducts vs. the chapel—which holds the true prize?  
- Sera slipped out a back door; Rik watches you; Tomas awaits payment and guidance.  

You hold the note, the barkeep watches, and the room waits for your next move.

conversation.content:
*You let the silence settle between you, the weight of her words pressing down like the stone overhead. Then you nod slowly.*

"Then we'd better make sure we don't end up in the sump."

*Sera snorts—a dry, humorless sound—and turns back toward the tunnel.*

"Stay sharp. We're not alone down here."

*She sets off again, her lantern casting long, dancing shadows across the wet walls. The tunnel opens into a wider chamber—an old cistern, long since drained. The floor is cracked, and in the center, a single shaft of gray light falls from a grate above, illuminating a spiral of graffiti on the stone floor.*

*It's the same mark as on the note.*

*Sera stops at the edge of the light, staring down at the spiral.*

"...Well. That's not ominous at all."

*She looks up at the grate, then back at you.*

"That leads to the street level, two blocks from the North Gate. The chapel is another quarter mile east, underground. There's an entrance through a collapsed wine cellar."

*She turns to face you fully, her expression unreadable in the half-light.*

"So. Last chance to walk away. Once we step into that light, we're committed."

**What do you do?**

1. **Step into the light** — "I didn't come this far to turn back now."
2. **Ask her a final question** — "Before we go, one thing... who hired Garrick? Who sent him down here in the first place?"
3. **Examine the spiral mark closely** — there might be more to this graffiti than meets the eye
4. **Suggest a different approach** — go to the bell tower first, scout the chapel from above before entering
Unknown:

*You climb up into the granary, taking in the space. The lantern light pushes back the shadows, revealing a small but organized camp. Maps are pinned to a sagging wall with rusted nails. A leather-bound journal sits on the table, its pages curling at the edges.*

*Sera watches you take it in. She pours water from a canteen into two tin cups and slides one across the table to you.*

"Garrick's journal," *she says, nodding at the book.* "Found it in a dry niche three days after they pulled him out. The sump wardens missed it—they were too busy trying to figure out how a man with no knife wounds drowned in ankle-deep water."

*She takes a sip of her water, her eyes distant.*

"He was onto something. Something big." *She taps the journal.* "He talks about a 'sealed door' with 'Imperial writing that hurts to look at.' A room beneath the old aqueducts that doesn't appear on any map."

*She looks at you, her gaze sharpening.*

"I think Vane sent me to find what Garrick found. And I think whoever wrote that note wants to make sure I don't bring it back to him."

*She pushes the journal across the table toward you.*

"Read the last three pages. Tell me if you think I'm crazy."
Unknown:

*The words land like a splash of cold water. Sera's hand freezes on her knife hilt. For a long moment, she just stares at you—then she exhales, a slow, shuddering breath.*

"Rik," *she says, the name coming out like a curse.* "The nervous little rat from the tavern."

*She looks down at the cask, then back at you, her expression shifting from suspicion to a grim, reluctant respect.*

"Alright. You've earned the full story."

*She sits down on a crate, the tension draining from her shoulders. The cask sits between you, its lid still slightly ajar.*

"Aldric Vane—the man who hired me—isn't just a curiosity shop owner. He's a collector of things that should stay buried. The dagger was his, yes. But the cask? That's something else entirely."

*She gestures at the iron-bound container.*

"Vane didn't send me for the dagger. He sent me for what's *in* the cask. The dagger was a test—a way to see if I could be trusted. The real job was to retrieve the cask and bring it to a meeting at the old chapel beneath the North Gate. He was supposed to meet me there, pay me the rest, and take possession."

*She looks at you, her eyes hard.*

"But someone else knows about the arrangement. Someone who wants the cask for themselves—or wants to make sure Vane never gets it."

*She nods at the scrap in your hand.*

"Rik's been following me for weeks. I thought he was just a two-coin thug looking to shake me down. But if he's got the spiral scar... he's more than that. He's connected to whatever Vane is tangled in."

*She stands, picking up her pack.*

"So here's my offer: we take the cask to the chapel

*You crouch down, running your fingers over the pale, polished surface of the bone. The skull seems to stare back at you, its empty sockets filled with shadow.*

*You speak softly, almost to yourself.* "Who were you?"

*The echo of your voice fades into the tunnel. For a moment, nothing happens. Then—*

*A single drop of water falls from the ceiling, landing precisely in the center of the skull's forehead. It runs down the bone in a thin rivulet, tracing the line of a crack you hadn't noticed before.*
